rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al VS. CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT R.M.S. ASSOCIATES INC. D/B/A ROGER STONE INSURANCE, JOHN LOGUE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants did not breach a duty they owed to plaintiffs. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendants provided the insurance policy that plaintiffs requested, i.e., a claims made policy. Plaintiffs do not present any facts showing that defendants owed plaintiffs a broader duty. (Fitzpatrick v. Hayes (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 916, 927). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MADELYN KAHN VS. EUGENE M WOLF MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Defts Subpoena For Medical Records Of John Grollmus, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 8. PLAINTIFF MADELYN KAHN'S Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena For Medical Records Of John Grollmus, M.D. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT DENNIS SHEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Plaintiff's complaint is barred by the one-year statute of limitations set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 340.5. The letter to Dr. Shen dated July 4, 2009 served as a notice of intent to sue under Code of Civil Procedure 364 and rendered the second notice of intent to sue dated May 5, 2010 ineffective for tolling purposes. (Bennett v. Shahhal (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 384, 389). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, THE AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Site Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RUBEN RAMIREZ et al VS. ESTATE OF JUAN MARTINEZ MAGALLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT BRITE TRANSPORT SYSTEM, INC., DEL MONTE FOODS CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to causes of action 3, 4 and 6 and denied as to causes of action 1, 2, and 5. As to causes of action 3, 4, and 6, defendant satisfied its summary motion burden because plaintiff's special interrogatory responses regarding these claims are factually devoid and plaintiff failed to present sufficient evidence to create a triable issue. As to causes of action 1, 2 and 5, Brite was responsible for Mr. Loza's acts as a matter of law and there is a triable issue whether Brite acted as Del Monte's agent due to the amount of control Del Monte exercised over Brite. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied as moot as the Court granted relief from waiver of objections for excusable neglect and substantial compliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SMITH VS. BOSTON PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 13, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT BOSTON PROPERTIES, INC., BOSTON PROPERTIES MANAGEMENT, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Denied.
